quote_of_day.text: Lighter skin isn't a personality trait. They'd still have to be interesting. That shit jams your mind though so watch out. It's artificial. Matrix tricks.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather for Indus today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of ethnic taxonomy moved over the board, as one anon proposed a forced choice among several groups for deletion. The surrounding discussion was dense and argumentative.

A brief, enigmatic post sparked a minor exchange, remaining largely overcast. Meanwhile, a front of discontent about the lack of 'mudslime hate threads' blew in, bringing speculation about possible moderation influence.

In lighter moments, a scattered high-pressure system of friendly tech support developed, as one OP announced plans for a live streaming website and received suggestions.

Elsewhere, a lone post about being stuck in India hung in the air, a small, isolated fog bank.

articles.title: Anon Asked to Choose One Group to Delete
articles.body:
Anon opened a thread asking which group they would delete if given the option: Northwest Aryans, Mlecchas, Eastern Indians, or Dravidians. They were also asked to state their own ethnicity or region and give a reason for their choice.

Anon responded with various opinions on which group to delete.

One anon said they would choose to delete Mlecchas, calling them "shitty" and stating they could keep their "temples and cow piss." Another anon suggested deleting Dravidians, calling them "soyjak southies" who think they're the smartest.

Some anons expressed disinterest in the question, with one saying they'd delete all of them and be done with it. Another anon stated that Dravidians aren't Aryans, but a distinct people who adopted the Aryan religion.

The term "Mlecchas" was criticized, with one anon labeling it a slur. Others defended their preferred groups, with one stating that Dravidians have "some real history" compared to Eastern Indians.

Anons also joked about the question, with one suggesting they'd delete Northwest Aryans just to spite "incel normies." Another anon called the thread "retarded af" and said they'd delete none of the groups.

The discussion also touched on cultural identity, with one anon stating that Dravidians have a "shred of culture left" in the form of "gorehabba kek." Others disagreed, with one calling Dravidians "mlecchas with dumber memes." The thread was marked by a tone of mockery and disdain for various groups, with anons frequently using pejorative terms.

No clear consensus emerged on which group to delete, with responses ranging from apathy to hostility.

articles.title: Anon sparks brief exchange with enigmatic post
articles.body:
Anon kicked off the discussion with a simple 'here yo go.' One respondent was confused, asking 'literally how the hell am i supposed to respond to that.' The original poster received some attention, with one user saying 'Thanks for headpats anon saving this to my collection.' Another user made a comment about the original post being 'cute,' to which another user responded with 'randi.' The conversation also involved some references to other users and posts, including a mention of 'the flaggot who used to post bhangi kalej chronicles' and a question about 'qrd on SWOL?' Some users exchanged brief messages, with one saying 'maybe no' in response to another post.

A user also claimed to have made another thread, saying '>>20269 ananas I made the thread, thunder. Didn't bother namefagging while making it though.'

articles.title: Anon Laments Lack of Mudslime Hate Threads
articles.body:
Anon expressed concern that the board had too few 'mudslime hate threads' and wondered if this was a result of a new psychological operation by the JIDF, glowie, or RAW to normalize certain views. The poster questioned if moderators were compromised.

Another anon responded that the issue wasn't the discussion itself but rather that such topics often attracted low-IQ posters without factual arguments, which could overshadow peaceful discussions. This anon suggested it was better not to allow these discussions to begin with.

A respondent told the OP to 'shut up' and suggested that the board had simply had enough of the topic, which didn't need to be a conspiracy.

This anon advised the OP to 'go touch grass.' One poster joked about the possibility of the Indian government secretly reducing Muslim populations, finding humor in the idea that an event similar to the Bihar gobhi case could occur decades later.

A different anon claimed responsibility for removing 'religion and caste bait threads,' suggesting this might explain the lack of such discussions.

In a later response, an individual identifying as 'gay trans Dalit with no legs 2 peenor and one ball' posted a greeting and a name, 'Joy Bhem.' Another poster pointed out that there seemed to be more hatred towards LC than Muslims on the board.

The conversation also included a query about whether an image was AI-generated and a comment about some women's angelic appearance. An anon mocked the idea of being a janitor on a five-user board, implying the challenges of moderation.

articles.title: OP starts live streaming website, others offer suggestions
articles.body:
Anon announced plans to start work on a live streaming website after a five-month hiatus. The original poster mentioned doing karaoke with YouTube videos but finding it boring alone.

One respondent suggested creating a schedule like TV for a night walk and streaming from a mobile anonymously. Another anon recommended using Twitch instead of building a site from scratch.

Some anons offered words of encouragement, with one saying 'good job op' and another requesting updates on the site's progress.

articles.title: Stuck in India
articles.body:
Anon posted 'I'm stuck in India'. Another anon suggested trying Karnataka, jokingly calling it "safe" with the company of cows.

A respondent told the OP to get a grip and accused them of posting 'pedo shit' they couldn't deal with in their own life.

Anon also joked about the OP taking a motorcycle or just lying down in the street and dying. One user responded philosophically, noting aren't we all stuck in some form or other.
